Title: German (de) - UI Translation
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
- German language available in storefront language switcher
Steps:
[ACT] Open language switcher in header or footer and select German (de)
[WAIT] Wait for page to reload with de locale
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}} homepage
[ASSERT] Verify main navigation labels are in German
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/catalog (or equivalent category page)
[ASSERT] Verify page headings, filter labels, and button text are in German
[NAV] Navigate to a product detail page
[ASSERT] Verify Add to Cart button, product labels, and breadcrumb are in German
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/account (if accessible)
[ASSERT] Verify account menu labels, form field labels, and section headings are in German
Assertions:
[DOM] Language switcher shows "de" or German flag as active selection
[DOM] Main navigation items render in German (not English)
[DOM] CTA buttons (e.g., Add to Cart, Checkout) display German text
[DOM] Form labels and placeholders are in German
[STATE] No English fallback text visible anywhere in the UI
[DOM] No raw translation keys (e.g., translation.button.add) visible
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] No 404 errors for locale resource files (e.g., de.json)
[NETWORK] Locale file loaded successfully (HTTP 200)
Failure_Signals: English text visible in non-English UI;Translation key (e.g., translation.button.add) shown instead of text;Mixed-language UI (some elements in English some in German)
Cleanup: Switch language back to English (en) via language switcher

Title: English (en) - Baseline
Preconditions: - English language set (default storefront language)
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}} homepage with English locale active
[ASSERT] Verify English is the default language on first load
[NAV] Navigate through homepage, catalog, PDP, and account sections
[ASSERT] Verify all UI elements display readable English text
[ASSERT] Verify no raw translation keys are visible anywhere
[ASSERT] Verify no missing translation placeholders (empty labels or blank buttons)
Assertions:
[DOM] Language switcher shows English (en) as active by default
[DOM] All navigation items, buttons, and labels render in complete English
[STATE] No raw translation keys (e.g., translation.button.add) visible anywhere
[DOM] No blank or empty text nodes where translated content is expected
[DOM] All text is readable and grammatically complete
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] No 404 errors for en locale resource file
[NETWORK] en.json (or equivalent) locale file loads with HTTP 200
Failure_Signals: Translation key (e.g., translation.button.add) shown instead of text;Blank or empty labels where text is expected;English marked as unavailable in language switcher
Cleanup: No cleanup required — English is the default state

Title: Currency Switcher Visible
Preconditions: Store configured with multiple currencies enabled
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}} homepage
[ASSERT] Check the header area for a currency switcher control
[SCROLL] Scroll to the footer if currency switcher not found in header
[ASSERT] Verify currency switcher is present in header or footer
[ACT] Click or tap the currency switcher
[WAIT] Wait for the currency list to open
[ASSERT] Verify list of available currencies appears
[ASSERT] Verify the currently active currency is visually indicated as selected
[ASSERT] Verify all store-configured currencies are listed (minimum: USD, EUR)
[KEY] Press Tab to navigate to the currency switcher
[ASSERT] Verify the currency switcher is reachable and operable via keyboard
Assertions:
[DOM] Currency switcher control is visible in header or footer
[DOM] Clicking/tapping the switcher opens a list of available currencies
[STATE] Current active currency is shown as selected in the list
[DOM] All store-configured currencies listed — minimum USD and EUR present
[DOM] Currency switcher is reachable via keyboard Tab navigation and operable via Enter/Space
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] cart price query returns amount in selected currency
[CONSOLE] No JavaScript errors when opening currency switcher
Failure_Signals: Currency switcher not visible in header or footer;Switcher opens but shows empty list;Active currency not highlighted in the list;Currency switcher not keyboard accessible
Cleanup: Switch currency back to USD

Title: Switch to EUR
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
Store configured with USD and EUR, currently displaying USD
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to a product category page showing prices in USD
[ASSERT] Note a product price displayed in USD (e.g., $100.00) as baseline
[ACT] Open the currency switcher
[ACT] Select EUR from the currency list
[WAIT] Wait for prices to update on the current page
[ASSERT] Verify all product prices on the category page now display in EUR
[ASSERT] Verify the currency symbol changed from $ to the euro symbol
[NAV] Navigate to the cart page (if items are present in cart)
[ASSERT] Verify cart line item prices are in EUR
[ASSERT] Verify cart total is in EUR
[ASSERT] Verify mini-cart header shows EUR amounts
Assertions:
[DOM] All product prices on category page convert to EUR after selection
[DOM] Currency symbol changes from $ to euro symbol
[DOM] Cart line item prices and totals shown in EUR
[DOM] Mini-cart header reflects EUR amounts
[MATH] Exchange rate applied consistently — all prices on the page reflect the same rate
[STATE] USD prices no longer visible after switching to EUR
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] cart price query returns amount in selected currency
[CONSOLE] No JavaScript errors during currency switch
[NETWORK] No failed requests when prices are re-fetched in EUR
Failure_Signals: prices revert to USD after navigation;Currency symbol not updating after switch;EUR symbol not displayed (raw "EUR" text shown instead)
Cleanup: Switch currency back to USD

Title: Currency Symbol Display
Preconditions: Store configured with USD, EUR, and GBP currencies
Steps:
[ACT] Set currency to USD via the currency switcher
[NAV] Navigate to any product page
[ASSERT] Note the currency symbol and number format displayed (expected: $1,234.56)
[ACT] Set currency to EUR via the currency switcher
[NAV] Navigate to the same product page
[ASSERT] Note the euro symbol and number format displayed (expected: euro symbol with locale-appropriate formatting)
[ACT] Set currency to GBP via the currency switcher
[NAV] Navigate to the same product page
[ASSERT] Note the pound symbol and number format displayed (expected: pound symbol with locale-appropriate formatting)
[ASSERT] Compare symbol placement for each: verify correct prefix or suffix position per currency locale convention
[ASSERT] Verify no raw currency code (USD, EUR, GBP) is displayed as a substitute for the symbol
Assertions:
[FORMAT] USD: $ symbol as prefix, format $1,234.56
[FORMAT] EUR: euro symbol displayed, format follows locale convention (e.g., 1.234,56 euro symbol or euro symbol 1,234.56 depending on locale)
[FORMAT] GBP: pound symbol as prefix, format follows locale convention
[DOM] Symbol placement correct for each currency — prefix or suffix as per international convention
[DOM] No raw currency ISO code (USD/EUR/GBP) displayed instead of the currency symbol
[FORMAT] Decimal and thousands separators correct per currency locale for each of the three currencies
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] cart price query returns amount in selected currency
[CONSOLE] No JavaScript errors related to Intl.NumberFormat during currency formatting
[NETWORK] Currency symbol data loaded correctly for all three currencies
Failure_Signals: prices revert to USD after navigation;Raw currency code (e.g., USD) shown instead of symbol;Symbol on wrong side (e.g., 1,234.56$ instead of $1,234.56);Separators incorrect for the selected currency
Cleanup: Switch currency back to USD

Title: DE Locale — Category Permalink Direct URL Resolves to Correct Category
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
Storefront accessible at {{FRONT_URL}}; DE locale is available; at least one top-level category is reachable in EN (a category page at {{FRONT_URL}}/<slug> returns HTTP 200); DE locale category URL follows pattern {{FRONT_URL}}/de/<slug>
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/catalog
[WAIT] Category listing page loads; at least one category card/link is visible
[ACT] Click the first top-level category link in the category grid
[WAIT] Category page loads; URL settles
[ASSERT] Capture the URL path segment after {{FRONT_URL}} as SLUG_EN (e.g. '/accessories'). Verify SLUG_EN is non-empty and has no language prefix
[ASSERT] Note the category heading text displayed — store as CATEGORY_NAME_EN
[NAV] Navigate directly to {{FRONT_URL}}/de/<SLUG_EN> (replace <SLUG_EN> with the full captured slug, preserving all characters)
[WAIT] Page loads; wait for category heading to appear or error state to resolve
[ASSERT] Verify the page is not a 404: no 'not found' heading, HTTP response is 200
[ASSERT] Verify the category heading on the DE page matches the expected content for that category (same category as EN, either in German translation or same label if untranslated)
[ASSERT] Verify the language indicator shows 'de' or 'Deutsch' as the active locale
[ASSERT] Verify URL in browser address bar is exactly {{FRONT_URL}}/de/<SLUG_EN> — no redirect to a different slug, no characters altered
Assertions:
[NAV] URL is {{FRONT_URL}}/de/<SLUG_EN> (full slug preserved; no characters stripped from slug after /de/ prefix)
[DOM] Category page renders correctly: category heading visible and non-empty (not a 404 page)
[DOM] Language indicator shows 'de' or 'Deutsch' as the active locale
[STATE] Category content corresponds to the same category navigated to in EN — same category name or its translation
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] GET {{FRONT_URL}}/de/<SLUG_EN> returns HTTP 200 (not 404 or 301 to error)
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors when loading the DE-locale category URL directly
Failure_Signals: 404 on {{FRONT_URL}}/de/<SLUG_EN>; browser redirected away from the direct DE URL; category heading shows a different category than expected; language indicator still shows EN after direct DE URL navigation; console TypeError on page load
Cleanup: Navigate back to EN locale (click language switcher → select English); close any open panels

Title: Locale↔Slug Collision — Language Switcher on Deep Route Does Not Eat Slug Characters
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
Storefront accessible at {{FRONT_URL}}; at least two supported locales available in the language switcher (e.g. English and one 2-letter locale); STRUCTURAL GUARD: if no top-level category slug whose first 2 characters match a supported 2-letter locale code can be found at runtime, skip this case with a note in evidence — do not fail
Steps:
--- SETUP: discover a collision-eligible slug ---
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/catalog
[WAIT] Category listing page loads; at least one category card/link is visible
[ACT] browser_snapshot → enumerate all top-level category card link hrefs visible in the main content area
[ACT] Filter the captured hrefs: find the first slug (path segment after the last '/') whose first 2 characters case-insensitively match any entry in supported_2letter_codes (de|it|no|pl|fi|ru|es|pt|fr|sv|el). Store as COLLISION_SLUG (e.g. 'destinations' → first 2 chars 'de' matches 'de'). Store the matching locale code as COLLISION_LOCALE (e.g. 'de')
[ACT] If no COLLISION_SLUG found: log 'STRUCTURAL GUARD — no collision-eligible top-level slug present in catalog at this time; skipping case' to evidence and skip remaining steps
[ACT] Log COLLISION_SLUG and COLLISION_LOCALE to evidence before proceeding
--- FLOW: set active locale to the collision locale, then navigate to the deep route ---
[ACT] Ensure active locale is NOT COLLISION_LOCALE: if the language switcher currently shows COLLISION_LOCALE, first click the language switcher and select English (en) or en-GB; wait for page reload
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/catalog
[ACT] Click the top-level category whose slug is COLLISION_SLUG
[WAIT] Category page loads and URL settles; verify URL contains COLLISION_SLUG intact
[ASSERT] Store the full URL path as DEEP_ROUTE (e.g. '/en-GB/destinations' or '/destinations')
[ASSERT] Store the category heading text visible on the page as CATEGORY_HEADING_BEFORE_SWITCH
--- FLOW: switch language to COLLISION_LOCALE using the language switcher ---
[ACT] Locate the language switcher control in the header or footer
[ACT] Click the language switcher to open the locale list
[WAIT] Locale list is open and options are visible
[ACT] Click the option corresponding to COLLISION_LOCALE (e.g. 'Deutsch' for 'de', 'Italiano' for 'it')
[WAIT] Page reloads; wait for the page heading or main content to settle (up to 5 seconds)
[ASSERT] Capture the URL in the browser address bar as URL_AFTER_SWITCH
[ASSERT] Verify URL_AFTER_SWITCH contains the full COLLISION_SLUG without any characters eaten — e.g. if COLLISION_SLUG is 'destinations' and COLLISION_LOCALE is 'de', URL must contain 'destinations' not 'stinations'
[ASSERT] Verify URL_AFTER_SWITCH does NOT render a 404 page: page heading is not 'not found' / '404' / 'Seite nicht gefunden' or equivalent
[ASSERT] Verify the language indicator in header shows COLLISION_LOCALE as active
Assertions:
[NAV] URL after language switch contains the full COLLISION_SLUG intact (zero characters eaten from the slug)
[DOM] Category page renders correctly after switch: page heading is visible and non-empty; heading is not a '404' or 'not found' message
[DOM] Language indicator shows COLLISION_LOCALE as the active selection after the switch
[STATE] The category displayed after the switch is the same category as before the switch (same heading, or its translation in COLLISION_LOCALE)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] GET for the post-switch URL returns HTTP 200 (not 404)
[CONSOLE] No TypeError or routing errors in browser console after the language switch
Failure_Signals: URL after switch has slug characters eaten (e.g. '/de/stinations' instead of '/de/destinations'); 404 page shown after language switch; language indicator still shows the previous locale after the switch; category heading disappears or shows a different category after the switch; console TypeError on page load
Cleanup: Click the language switcher and select English (en) or en-GB to restore the original locale; navigate to {{FRONT_URL}} homepage

Title: Locale↔Slug Collision — Direct Address-Bar Navigation Does Not Eat Slug Characters When Active Locale Matches Slug Prefix
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
Storefront accessible at {{FRONT_URL}}; at least two supported locales available; STRUCTURAL GUARD: if no top-level category slug whose first 2 characters match a supported 2-letter locale code can be found at runtime, skip this case with a note in evidence — do not fail
Steps:
--- SETUP: discover a collision-eligible slug (same recipe as L10N-ROUTE-001) ---
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/catalog
[WAIT] Category listing page loads
[ACT] browser_snapshot → enumerate all top-level category card link hrefs visible in the main content area
[ACT] Filter hrefs: find the first slug whose first 2 characters match any entry in supported_2letter_codes. Store as COLLISION_SLUG; store the matching code as COLLISION_LOCALE
[ACT] If no COLLISION_SLUG found: log 'STRUCTURAL GUARD — no collision-eligible slug present; skipping case' and skip remaining steps
[ACT] Log COLLISION_SLUG and COLLISION_LOCALE to evidence
--- CONTROL: verify the slug resolves correctly when active locale does NOT equal COLLISION_LOCALE ---
[ACT] Ensure active locale is NOT COLLISION_LOCALE: if needed, open language switcher and select English (en) or en-GB; wait for reload
[NAV] Navigate directly to {{FRONT_URL}}/<COLLISION_SLUG> (no locale prefix; replace <COLLISION_SLUG> with the captured slug)
[WAIT] Page loads; wait for heading or error state to settle
[ASSERT] Capture URL in address bar as CONTROL_URL. Verify page renders a category (not 404) and heading is non-empty. Store heading as CONTROL_HEADING
--- SUT: activate COLLISION_LOCALE, then navigate the same non-prefixed slug via address bar ---
[ACT] Open language switcher
[ACT] Click the language switcher to open the locale list
[WAIT] Locale list is open
[ACT] Select COLLISION_LOCALE (e.g. Deutsch for de)
[WAIT] Page reloads with COLLISION_LOCALE active; language indicator updated
[NAV] Type {{FRONT_URL}}/<COLLISION_SLUG> directly in the address bar and press Enter (no locale prefix in the typed URL)
[WAIT] Page loads; wait for heading or error state to settle
[ASSERT] Capture URL in address bar as SUT_URL
[ASSERT] Verify SUT_URL contains the full COLLISION_SLUG (no chars eaten) — SUT_URL must include COLLISION_SLUG intact, e.g. '/de/destinations' not '/de/stinations'
[ASSERT] Verify the page is NOT a 404: heading is not 'not found' / '404' / equivalent
[ASSERT] Verify the category heading corresponds to the same category as CONTROL_HEADING (same name or its COLLISION_LOCALE translation)
Assertions:
[NAV] SUT_URL contains the full COLLISION_SLUG without character truncation
[DOM] Page renders a valid category page (not 404) after direct navigation with the active locale matching the slug's leading chars
[DOM] Category heading is non-empty and corresponds to the same category as the control nav
[DOM] Language indicator shows COLLISION_LOCALE as active
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] GET for SUT_URL returns HTTP 200
[CONSOLE] No TypeError or routing errors in browser console during direct navigation
Failure_Signals: SUT_URL shows eaten slug chars (e.g. '/de/stinations'); 404 page shown; different category displayed compared to control nav; console TypeError on load
Cleanup: Open language switcher and select English (en) or en-GB; navigate to {{FRONT_URL}} homepage

Title: Cross-Locale Explicit Deep-Link Does Not Stack Prefixes — Incoming /{locale}/ Switches Active Locale Instead of Prepending
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
Storefront accessible at {{FRONT_URL}}; at least two 2-letter locale codes available (e.g. de and en or en-US); four route types accessible: home, a PDP, a category listing, and search
Steps:
--- SETUP: establish a non-default active locale ---
[ACT] Open language switcher in the header or footer
[ACT] Click the language switcher to open the locale list
[WAIT] Locale list is open
[ACT] Select Deutsch (de) from the locale list
[WAIT] Page reloads; language indicator shows 'de' / 'Deutsch'
[ASSERT] Verify active locale is now de (language indicator confirms)
--- SUB-FLOW A: explicit /{incoming_locale}/ home deep link ---
[NAV] Navigate directly to {{FRONT_URL}}/en/ in the address bar
[WAIT] Page loads; wait for main content to settle
[ASSERT] Capture URL as URL_A and page heading/language-indicator as LOCALE_A
[ASSERT] Verify URL_A does NOT contain '/de/en/' (prefix-stack pattern)
[ASSERT] Verify page is not a 404
[ASSERT] Verify language indicator shows 'en' or English as the now-active locale (locale switched, not stacked)
--- SUB-FLOW B: explicit /{incoming_locale}/ + category deep link ---
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/catalog
[WAIT] Category page loads
[ACT] Click any top-level category card
[WAIT] Category page loads; URL settles
[ACT] Capture the slug portion of the URL (the path after the locale prefix, e.g. '/accessories') as CAT_SLUG
[NAV] Set active locale back to de: open language switcher → select Deutsch → wait for reload
[NAV] Navigate directly to {{FRONT_URL}}/en/<CAT_SLUG> in the address bar (explicit en prefix)
[WAIT] Page loads; wait for heading or error state to settle
[ASSERT] Capture URL as URL_B
[ASSERT] Verify URL_B does NOT contain '/de/en/' (prefix-stack pattern)
[ASSERT] Verify page is not a 404
[ASSERT] Verify language indicator shows 'en' or English (locale switched to en, not stacked)
--- SUB-FLOW C: explicit /{incoming_locale}/ + search query deep link ---
[NAV] Set active locale back to de: open language switcher → select Deutsch → wait for reload
[NAV] Navigate directly to {{FRONT_URL}}/en/search?q=laptop in the address bar
[WAIT] Page loads; wait for search results heading to settle
[ASSERT] Capture URL as URL_C
[ASSERT] Verify URL_C does NOT contain '/de/en/' prefix-stack pattern
[ASSERT] Verify page is not a 404
[ASSERT] Verify language indicator shows 'en' or English as active locale
Assertions:
[NAV] None of URL_A, URL_B, URL_C contain a double-prefix stacking pattern (e.g. '/de/en/', '/fr/de/', or any /{localeA}/{localeB}/ pattern)
[DOM] Pages for sub-flows A, B, and C render valid content — not a 404 for any sub-flow
[DOM] Language indicator shows the incoming locale ('en') as active after each explicit deep-link navigation — confirming locale switch occurred rather than stacking
[STATE] Active locale changed to the incoming locale on all three sub-flows
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] HTTP responses for URL_A, URL_B, URL_C are all HTTP 200
[CONSOLE] No TypeError or routing errors in browser console during any of the three sub-flow navigations
Failure_Signals: URL contains double-prefix stacking (e.g. '/de/en/'); 404 page shown on any sub-flow; language indicator still shows 'de' after navigating to an explicit '/en/' URL (no switch occurred); console TypeError on any sub-flow load
Cleanup: Open language switcher and select English (en) or en-GB; navigate to {{FRONT_URL}} homepage to restore default locale

Title: Post-Prefix Boundary Fuzz — Trailing Slash, Percent-Encoded Slash, Fragment, and Double Slash After Locale Prefix Are Handled Gracefully
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
Storefront accessible at {{FRONT_URL}}; DE locale is available; a valid category slug is resolvable at runtime
Steps:
--- SETUP: capture a valid category slug ---
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/catalog
[WAIT] Category listing page loads; at least one category card visible
[ACT] browser_snapshot → capture the href of the first top-level category card link in main content; extract the slug-only portion (path after locale prefix if present, e.g. 'accessories') as VALID_SLUG
[ACT] Log VALID_SLUG to evidence before proceeding
--- SETUP: activate DE locale ---
[ACT] Open language switcher
[ACT] Click the language switcher to open the locale list
[WAIT] Locale list is open
[ACT] Select Deutsch (de)
[WAIT] Page reloads; language indicator shows de
--- VARIANT 1: trailing slash after slug ---
[NAV] Navigate directly to {{FRONT_URL}}/de/<VALID_SLUG>/ (note the trailing slash)
[WAIT] Page loads; wait for heading or error state to settle
[ASSERT] Capture URL as URL_V1 and note whether a category heading is visible or a 404 is shown
[ASSERT] Verify no chars are eaten from VALID_SLUG in URL_V1
[ASSERT] Verify page is NOT a blank/crash state (either a valid category page OR a graceful redirect/normalisation to the canonical URL is acceptable; what is NOT acceptable is a JS exception or a blank page)
--- VARIANT 2: percent-encoded slash (%2F) after the locale prefix ---
[NAV] Navigate directly to {{FRONT_URL}}/de%2F<VALID_SLUG> (percent-encoded slash replaces the literal slash between locale and slug)
[WAIT] Page loads; wait for heading or error state to settle
[ASSERT] Capture URL as URL_V2
[ASSERT] Verify no server-level 5xx is returned; HTTP response is not 500 or 502
[ASSERT] Verify no unhandled JS exception in console
--- VARIANT 3: fragment identifier after locale prefix ---
[NAV] Navigate directly to {{FRONT_URL}}/de/<VALID_SLUG>#section1
[WAIT] Page loads; wait for heading or error state to settle
[ASSERT] Capture URL as URL_V3
[ASSERT] Verify no chars are eaten from VALID_SLUG (URL must contain VALID_SLUG intact before the '#')
[ASSERT] Verify page is not a 404
--- VARIANT 4: double slash after locale prefix ---
[NAV] Navigate directly to {{FRONT_URL}}/de//<VALID_SLUG> (double slash)
[WAIT] Page loads; wait for heading or error state to settle
[ASSERT] Capture URL as URL_V4
[ASSERT] Verify no unhandled JS exception or blank page; either a valid page or a graceful 404 with the storefront 404 component (not a browser-level crash)
[ASSERT] Verify URL_V4 does NOT show char-eating (VALID_SLUG intact in the URL if slug is present at all)
Assertions:
[DOM] None of the four variant URLs produce a JS exception / blank-page crash state
[NAV] VALID_SLUG chars are intact in URL_V1, URL_V3, URL_V4 wherever the slug appears (no char-eating)
[DOM] Variant 1 (trailing slash): either renders the correct category OR normalises to the canonical URL — not a JS crash
[DOM] Variant 2 (%2F): no 5xx server error; graceful response (browser-level URL rejection or normalisation is acceptable)
[DOM] Variant 3 (#fragment): correct category renders; fragment does not corrupt the path
[DOM] Variant 4 (double slash): either renders correctly or shows a graceful storefront 404 — not a blank page or JS exception
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] No 5xx responses for any of the four variant navigations
[CONSOLE] No TypeError or unhandled promise rejection in browser console for any variant
Failure_Signals: Any variant URL shows chars eaten from VALID_SLUG; blank white page (SPA crash) on any variant; HTTP 5xx for any variant; unhandled TypeError logged to console for any variant
Cleanup: Open language switcher and select English (en) or en-GB; navigate to {{FRONT_URL}} homepage
